title: Wrong inner join result
description:
## Bug Report

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
```
CREATE TABLE t1(c1 DOUBLE , c2 BOOL);


INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (0.5, false);
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (0.5, false);


SELECT *
FROM t1
         inner join t1 as t0 ON ((t1.c2) <= (CAST(t1.c1 AS DATE)));

```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
In MySQL 8.0
```

empty

```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
```

0.5, 0, 0.5, 0
0.5, 0, 0.5, 0
0.5, 0, 0.5, 0
0.5, 0, 0.5, 0


```


### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
```
Release Version: v9.0.0-beta.1.pre-389-g21f9bb7b7a
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 21f9bb7b7a7478d825c4dee4fbf2ce913e3e7cf1
Git Branch: HEAD
UTC Build Time: 2025-03-11 09:37:34
GoVersion: go1.23.7
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: tikv
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(c1 INT, c2 TIMESTAMP, c3 BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO t2 VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 00:00:00', TRUE);
INSERT INTO t2 VALUES (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-02 00:00:00', FALSE);
SELECT * FROM t2 INNER JOIN t2 AS t0 ON (t2.c3 <= (CAST(t0.c1 AS TIMESTAMP)));
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE c2 IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Inner join on boolean and date cast comparison
